title: Chapter 10 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Advanced Kotlin Architecture!

questions.question: If you rigorously need to restrict a variable to EXACTLY one of three possible static statuses categorically (`DRAFT`, `PUBLISHED`, `DELETED`), what architectural structure structurally explicitly unconditionally solves this best natively?
questions.explanation: Enums explicitly mandate developers unequivocally select strictly from a locked, pre-approved finite catalog intrinsically.

questions.question: If an arrogant developer actively attempts to literally type `status = Status.DRFT` (missing the 'A'), what exactly happens logically?
options: The strictly-typed compiler universally blocks it explicitly natively.
questions.explanation: The explicit beauty essentially of Enums natively lies exactly unconditionally in providing 100% compile-time typo immunity globally.
